title: dysoxylum excelsum
intro: Dysoxylum excelsum, also known as the Philippine Makopa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae, native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dysoxylum excelsum include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Dysoxylum excelsum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and bark can be used to make herbal teas or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
